title: [apiv2] failed to export an running container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080

2. ./bin/podman run --name myalpine -td docker.io/library/alpine:latest /bin/sh

3. curl -v -s http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/myalpine/export

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080 &
[1] 96771
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ This function is EXPERIMENTAL.
ERRO[21692] unable to copy to response: "write tcp 127.0.0.1:8080->127.0.0.1:51110: write: connection reset by peer"

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ curl -v -s http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/myalpine/export
*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> GET /v1.40/libpod/containers/myalpine/export HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
>
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/octet; charset=us-ascii
< Date: Fri, 15 May 2020 09:28:56 GMT
< Transfer-Encoding: chunked
<
* Failed writing body (0 != 16384)
* Failed reading the chunked-encoded stream
* Closing connection 0
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
can successfully export an container content to specified path.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            2.0.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
Git Commit:         4611ff5ce4ff67a142363ab66e10944bfc40f860
Built:              Fri May 15 03:26:27 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.6.10-300.fc32.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 1165754368
  memTotal: 4118323200
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.13-2.fc32.x86_64

    version: |-
      crun version 0.13
      commit: e79e4de4ac16da0ce48777afb72c6241de870525
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.0.0-1.fc32.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.0.0
      commit: a3be729152a33e692cd28b52f664defbf2e7810a
      libslirp: 4.2.0
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 97h 19m 48.61s (Approximately 4.04 days)

```

```
4611ff5ce4ff67a142363ab66e10944bfc40f860
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Openstack VM

comments.body: this is not a bug, be sure you are using the proper method from the documentation which in this case is GET.  Add -X GET to your curl command.

> this is not a bug, be sure you are using the proper method from the documentation which in this case is GET. Add -X GET to your curl command.

Unnecessary use of -X or --request, GET is already inferred, a failure reason is users must use -o or redirect their output w/ curl, I can't find this in documentation - http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/libpodExportContainer , thanks.
